executive_summary: Latest updates from 2 sources covering the US-Israel-Iran conflict. Monitoring 2 key developments. AI analysis refreshing shortly.
execSummaryRich: Escalation: HIGH. Key developments: Netanyahu says Israel expanding buffer zone in southern Lebanon. Gaza toddler released from Israeli custody with ‘cigarette burn’ wounds. Situation remains active.

key_developments.headline: Netanyahu says Israel expanding buffer zone in southern Lebanon
key_developments.detail: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Israeli forces are expanding a “buffer zone” in southern Lebanon as operations against Hezbollah continue. “We have created a genuine security zone preventing any infiltration toward the Galilee and the northern border,” he said in a video statement. key_developments.headline: Gaza toddler released from Israeli custody with ‘cigarette burn’ wounds
key_developments.detail: A Palestinian toddler was returned from a 10-hour detention by Israeli forces in the Gaza Strip with apparent cigarette burns on his thighs, according to medical reports. terminology_explained.simple_explanation: Iran's elite Revolutionary Guard — controls missiles and drones.
Narrow waterway — 20% of world oil passes through it.

what_to_watch_next: Monitor live feeds for breaking developments.

india_impact.headline: India Monitors Gulf Conflict
india_impact.detail: India's energy security tied to Gulf stability.
india_impact.full_detail: India imports 85% of crude oil from Gulf. Strait of Hormuz disruption would immediately impact supply.

indiaSummary: India continues to monitor the US-Israel-Iran conflict closely given its significant dependence on Gulf energy imports and the large Indian diaspora. Oil price volatility directly impacts petrol prices for Indian consumers.
